description_long: This object is a fantastical cluster of large, irregularly shaped mushrooms with thick, textured stems and bulbous caps. The caps vary in size and color, ranging from deep brown to reddish hues, and some display a slightly wrinkled or rough surface. Several smaller mushrooms sprout from the base and along the stems, while a few thin, elongated stalks rise above the main cluster, topped with smaller caps. The stems are robust and appear fibrous, with a mottled, earthy coloration and knobby protrusions. The overall structure is sprawling and organic, with the mushrooms growing in a dense, interconnected group. The surface of the caps and stems appears matte and rough, suggesting a natural, unpolished texture.
description: A sprawling, dense cluster of large, brownish mushrooms with thick, textured stems and bulbous, rough-surfaced caps. Smaller mushrooms and knobby protrusions accent the organic, earthy structure.
description_view_0: From this angle, the largest mushroom caps and the thick, angled stems with knobby growths are prominent, with several smaller mushrooms visible at the base.
description_view_1: This view highlights the rounded backs of the main caps and shows the cluster's dense, interconnected base, with more of the smaller mushrooms visible.
description_view_2: Seen from above, the elongated stems and the spread of the mushroom caps are clear, with the smaller mushrooms and stalks standing out in the cluster.
description_view_3: This perspective emphasizes the sprawling nature of the stems and the underside of some caps, with the rough, matte texture of the surfaces more apparent.
